subject: ICYMI: Bill McSwain to Spend $6.8M on Campaign Ads in GOP Primary
body:
McSwain launches multi-media outreach effort, including $6.8M spent on TV ads alone | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us5.campaign-archive.com/?e=b1ec95759b&u=b9af0aef3fa3167622552aedb&id=0e19a15125)
---
| |
---
| **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE**
---
**February 3, 2022
CONTACT: Rachel Tripp
202.738.9089**
---
| |
# ICYMI: In the News - Bill McSwain to Spend $6.8M on Campaign Ads in GOP Primary

**West Chester, Pa. --** Bill McSwain, Republican candidate for Governor, on Monday launched the first in a series of television ads aimed at sharing his message of safety, security, and prosperity with voters across the Commonwealth. The ad is just the first step in a multi-million dollar outreach strategy executed in collaboration with Commonwealth Partners Chamber of Entrepreneurs' associated PACs. In addition to $6.8M spent on cable and broadcast ads alone, McSwain plans to expand his digital, direct mail, and radio presence through the May 17 primary.

The bold initiative is making headlines across Pennsylvania, select excerpts below:
---
| **_Delaware Valley Journal: Gubernatorial Candidate Bill McSwain to Spend $6.8 M on Campaign Ads in GOP Primary_** Bill McSwain, the former U.S. Attorney who is running for governor, is spending $6.8 million on cable and broadcast television, radio, and internet ads in the run-up to the May 17 Republican primary, according to filings. That is more than many of his opponents in the crowded GOP field have in their campaign coffers, let alone their advertising budgets. Indeed, Senate Pro Tempore Jake Corman’s campaign announced Monday it had raised $3 million and had $2.7 million cash on hand. Garnering the backing of the endorsement of Commonwealth Partners Chamber of Entrepreneurs, a free-market advocacy group, was a major coup for McSwain. “We’re confident that as voters meet Bill McSwain and learn about his record, they’ll conclude he’s the best choice to serve as Pennsylvania’s next governor,” said Matt Brouillette, Treasurer of Commonwealth Leaders Fund, a political action committee affiliated with Commonwealth Partners. “Bill is very honored to be able to share his message,” said Rachel Tripp, McSwain’s campaign spokeswoman. “With the combination of the Commonwealth Partners support and over 10,000 donors, this is the start of a long-term strategy.” The advertisements allow voters “share his vision,” she said. “This is just the beginning of a longer fight.” Indeed the winner of the primary will almost certainly face Democrat Josh Shapiro, the state Attorney General who is running for governor and who just received his party’s endorsement last weekend. Shapiro, who has no primary challengers, will be well-funded for the general election.
However, it might not be smooth sailing for the former Marine. “Having those kinds of resources is an undoubted benefit that allows a candidate to be competitive, but alone it is probably not sufficient to win,” said Berwood Yost, director of the Floyd Institute for Public Policy and Center for Opinion Research at Franklin & Marshall College. “The strategy here may be to build momentum in order to attract more donors and perhaps scare off a few other potential candidates. Of course, candidates are not likely to win in this state based on advertising alone, so we will need to see how his campaign does organizing on the ground. His performance in the straw polls suggests he has some work to do with party insiders, which signals a disconnect with the local party leaders who are important in these primary elections.” McSwain’s first television ad began airing on Fox News Monday. The commercial, entitled “Send In a Marine,” an announcer tells viewers McSwain served his country as a “scout sniper” in the Marine Corps and was appointed by former President Donald Trump as U.S. Attorney General for Southeastern Pennsylvania. In that role, he “put hard criminals behind bars and took down corrupt politicians.” The spot then pivots to tell the audience that as governor, McSwain would rebuild the economy, “reduce taxes on hardworking families,” and “empower parents to give their kids a great education.” McSwain, 52, grew up in West Chester, where he still lives and graduated from Yale and Harvard Law School. He is married to his childhood sweetheart, Stephanie. They have four children. After leaving the U.S. Attorney’s office, he joined the Duane Morris law firm.

_This piece was originally published 02/01/22, [available here](https://freedompapac.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9af0aef3fa3167622552aedb&id=d103eb01be&e=b1ec95759b). _
---
|
---
| _**Pennsylvania Capital-Star: The 2022 candidates for Pa. governor are raising big money for a big race**_ In the wide-open GOP primary to face Shapiro, primary voters are about to be bombarded with millions of dollars in ads backing GOP hopeful Bill McSwain. McSwain, a former federal prosecutor, has[ done poorly in early polling](https://freedompapac.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9af0aef3fa3167622552aedb&id=abe70cc373&e=b1ec95759b). But last month, he secured a key and deep-pocketed endorsement from Commonwealth Partners Chamber of Entrepreneurs, a[ free-market advocacy group](https://freedompapac.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9af0aef3fa3167622552aedb&id=9c7cccc0a2&e=b1ec95759b) mostly funded by one donor. Political groups associated with Commonwealth Partners already have [at least $20 million](https://freedompapac.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9af0aef3fa3167622552aedb&id=d946513c3b&e=b1ec95759b) in the bank. And the first round of spending — nearly $7 million on pro-McSwain TV ads — went out the door this week through an associated group, the Commonwealth Leaders Fund. The multi-million dollar buy will run through at least April, veteran conservative activist Matt Brouillette, who plays a role in both groups, told the Capital-Star. That spending will be buttressed by additional mail and digital ads, though Brouillette declined to elaborate on the planned totals for other mediums. Still, “we’ve got more if necessary,” Brouillette added. Commonwealth Partners receives most of its money from suburban Philadelphia billionaire [hedge fund manager Jeff Yass](https://freedompapac.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9af0aef3fa3167622552aedb&id=29f76fa66d&e=b1ec95759b), who is among the biggest political spenders in the country. He was [a top donor](https://freedompapac.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9af0aef3fa3167622552aedb&id=7ee95bb678&e=b1ec95759b) to the Club for Growth, a national group that has backed conservative U.S. Senators such as Ted Cruz, of Texas, and Josh Hawley of Missouri, though Yass has [tried to distance himself](https://freedompapac.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9af0aef3fa3167622552aedb&id=8c8dad1b4a&e=b1ec95759b) from their attempts to overturn the 2020 election results. McSwain’s spokesperson, Rachel Tripp, told the Capital-Star in an email that the spending “is the kind of comprehensive, long-term presence that our GOP opponents just can’t compete with, and reflects the aggressive commitment that will help Bill defeat Josh Shapiro.” Borick agreed. He compared the strategy to that of incumbent Gov. Tom Wolf, who [tapped ](https://freedompapac.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9af0aef3fa3167622552aedb&id=063fd77004&e=b1ec95759b)into his personal wealth to run a wave of ads in the 2014 Democratic primary and leapfrog over more notable state politicians. Facing a number of former elected officials, including Senate President Pro Tempore Jake Corman, R-Centre, and former U.S. Rep. Lou Barletta, of Hazelton, McSwain is in a similar position. “How do you make [McSwain] a clear threat in this race? it’s by building that name recognition,” Borick said, “and you do by spending a lot of money.” Other candidates, most of whom also [interviewed](https://freedompapac.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9af0aef3fa3167622552aedb&id=6d0744e30f&e=b1ec95759b) for Commonwealth Partner’s endorsement, could have trouble surmounting the pro-McSwain advertising blitz. And for some, the heavy spending will create tension about if McSwain and his backers are trying to buy the primary. “You have to wonder now who’s running for governor — Bill McSwain or Commonwealth Partners,” one GOP political operative involved in the governor’s campaign told the Capital-Star. In an email, Tripp said that McSwain has “a lifelong reputation for integrity and honesty,” and that, ”unlike many career politicians, he will never allow outside influences to interfere with his protection and defense of law-abiding citizens.” Asked about fundraising at a gubernatorial candidate forum held by [the Blair County GOP](https://freedompapac.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9af0aef3fa3167622552aedb&id=9d55ae498c&e=b1ec95759b) last weekend, McSwain said that Commonwealth Partners was willing to spend $20 million on his behalf after the endorsement. “If you have the best message, you’re going to have the most money,” McSwain said. “And I believe that I have the best message, and I definitely have the most money in this campaign.” _This piece was originally published 02/01/22, full article[available here](https://freedompapac.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9af0aef3fa3167622552aedb&id=8d83dd49fa&e=b1ec95759b)._
---
|
---
| McSwain's first television ad is currently live. Check it out below:
---
| [ ](https://freedompapac.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9af0aef3fa3167622552aedb&id=c68607ae13&e=b1ec95759b)
---
|
---
| **BACKGROUND: **
Appointed by President Trump, McSwain's record as US Attorney demonstrates his fierce defense of constitutional rights, dedication to upholding law and order, and history of putting criminals and corrupt politicians in jail. Prior to becoming an attorney, Bill served as a scout/sniper platoon commander in the U.S. Marine Corps. He was born in Philadelphia and raised in Chester County, where he lives today with his wife, Stephanie, and their four children. More information on McSwain's background and platform is available [here.](https://freedompapac.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9af0aef3fa3167622552aedb&id=2c77d6592c&e=b1ec95759b)
###

body: Don't miss the latest from the On Watch Podcast! [Action] Chris Farrell's On Watch Podcast: USMC Lt Col Stu Scheller & Accountability for the Afghanistan Collapse [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57807&L=26715&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY2hyaXMtZmFycmVsbHMtb24td2F0Y2gvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249YWN0aW9uK2FsZXJ0JnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=] CLICK ABOVE TO WATCH NOW! Former Lieutenant Colonel Stuart Scheller was an officer in the U.S. Marine Corps who spoke out on social media seeking accountability from senior military officers for the deadly, botched Afghanistan withdrawal in August 2021. Scheller was subsequently fired as battalion commander, thrown into the brig as a "flight risk," and then court-martialed. A veteran of the Iraq and Afghanistan wars, he was eventually given a “general discharge under honorable conditions.” This is his story. name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: When the lights went out
body:
Greg Abbott failed Texas. If you agree, I hope you'll consider making a split donation to our campaign and Donna Imam. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41405656/328690225/-573335974?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NjQ2OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MmM3ODk1OC02Mjg1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sI3RoZAM92vSvrow5Fay4NroLjxyT0HHNaD5OOGB6u0=)
Dear Friend, Where were you when the lights went out in Texas? For millions across this state, not only did last February's power grid failure plunge their homes into darkness, but their heaters stopped working, their pipes froze, and, unable to charge their phones or use their computers, they lost the ability to reach the outside world. It wasn’t an act of God, nor the incredible force of Mother Nature. It wasn’t a failure of turbines or technicians. This was wholly the fault of the governor, the man who had been repeatedly warned of vulnerabilities in the grid and who did nothing. The man who, instead of being with Texans in their moment of need, was on Fox News blaming the Green New Deal. Greg Abbott failed Texas.**[If you agree, I hope you'll consider making a split donation to our campaign and Donna Imam to defeat Greg Abbott, and help power our campaign for all Texans forward.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41405658/328690227/-571066549?refcode=pl_20220203_02&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NjQ2OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MmM3ODk1OC02Mjg1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sI3RoZAM92vSvrow5Fay4NroLjxyT0HHNaD5OOGB6u0=)** Abbott failed to prepare us for a completely preventable disaster, and then, after millions lost power and hundreds died because they froze to death in the energy capital of the world, he failed to make changes that would protect us from the next extreme weather event. That might be because his largest political donors profited from the suffering of Texans. Kelcy Warren’s company, for example, made over $2.4 billion on the grid failure. He wrote Abbott a $1 million check soon after. And to add insult to injury, Abbott forced the ratepayers to clean up his mess. Every Texas home will have to pay the Abbott Tax – an increase of at least $20-50 per month on our utility bills for the coming decades. For the next two weeks, I will drive to the communities in Texas that bore the brunt of Abbott’s failure. I’ll meet with those who found a way to come through, and hear from those who lost loved ones that didn’t. I’ll learn from everyday Texans who stepped up to lead in their communities when their own governor refused to. And I’ll lay out the steps I will take to make sure this never happens again. This is a drive to keep the lights on in Texas. To make sure we have a government that works for, and is accountable to, everyone in this state. [**Your support today for our campaign and Donna Imam will ensure that we can talk to as many Texans as possible over these next two weeks and offer our vision for a brighter Texas.** ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41405660/328690229/-571066549?refcode=pl_20220203_02&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NjQ2OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MmM3ODk1OC02Mjg1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sI3RoZAM92vSvrow5Fay4NroLjxyT0HHNaD5OOGB6u0=) | [Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41405661/328690231/-571066549?refcode=pl_20220203_02&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NjQ2OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MmM3ODk1OC02Mjg1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sI3RoZAM92vSvrow5Fay4NroLjxyT0HHNaD5OOGB6u0=)

name: Team Cranley-Fedor
subject: Today’s anniversary
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Cranley Fedor for Ohio Committee
body:
While the right to vote is the foundational principle of this nation, it is always one we have had to fight to realize. | Today, we celebrate 152 years of the 15th Amendment, friend. On this day in 1870, Black men and men of color were finally granted the right to cast a ballot — though they were all too often prevented from voting until the civil rights movement defeated racist Jim Crow laws a century later. Women of any race would not be enfranchised for another 50 years. **While the right to vote is the foundational principle of this nation, it is always one we have had to fight to realize.** And it’s a fight we can’t afford to lose now, as far-right legislators across the country seek to deny some of us access to the ballot box. Here in Ohio, GOP legislators want to limit mail-in voting, purge voter rolls, prohibit the use of ballot drop boxes, shorten early voting, and more. **It’s unacceptable, friend.** This Black History Month, John and Teresa ask you to join them in recommitting to the promise of a nation governed by and for the people, with liberty and justice for all. _That starts by defending and expanding voting rights — not making it harder for some people to cast a ballot._ **[Sign our petition to stand with John and Teresa and condemn Ohio Republicans’ anti-voting legislation. clean_body: While the right to vote is the foundational principle of this nation, it is always one we have had to fight to realize. | Today, we celebrate 152 years of the 15th Amendment, friend. On this day in 1870, Black men and men of color were finally granted the right to cast a ballot — though they were all too often prevented from voting until the civil rights movement defeated racist Jim Crow laws a century later. Women of any race would not be enfranchised for another 50 years. While the right to vote is the foundational principle of this nation, it is always one we have had to fight to realize. And it’s a fight we can’t afford to lose now, as far-right legislators across the country seek to deny some of us access to the ballot box. Here in Ohio, GOP legislators want to limit mail-in voting, purge voter rolls, prohibit the use of ballot drop boxes, shorten early voting, and more. It’s unacceptable, friend. This Black History Month, John and Teresa ask you to join them in recommitting to the promise of a nation governed by and for the people, with liberty and justice for all. That starts by defending and expanding voting rights — not making it harder for some people to cast a ballot. With reproductive freedom under attack in much of the country, Illinois remains a safe haven state for women to access reproductive health care. I’m proud of the steps Illinois has taken to make abortion affordable and accessible for all women, _but we can’t take this progress for granted._

**Last year, the five Republicans in the Illinois congressional delegation signed on to a legal brief asking the Supreme Court to overturn _Roe v. Wade_.**

And this is just the tip of the iceberg. Illinois Republicans are determined to undo decades of progress, and I know they won’t stop until they succeed.

I’m running for Congress to serve as the steadfast advocate IL-13 needs for a woman’s right to choose, so that _all_ women, in Illinois and around the country, are able to access the health care services they need.

| ICYMI, Gail Huff Brown Pens Op-Ed: It’s time for Big Tech to make some big changes
---
| | | **
** In case you missed it, yesterday Gail Huff Brown penned an Op-Ed in The Boston Herald criticizing Big Tech for their censorship of conservative voices, and highlighted the need for dramatic change moving forward. [
](https://click.mlsend.com/link/c/YT0xODc3Nzk1OTA3NTU1MDQwMDEzJmM9ZDZyMiZlPTAmYj04OTQ1MjY4MzgmZD15NW01aTV5.4l5WY9RTu8oTKOqK9u4u81Vs06Ohcc1V33ek8oAvIdI) [**Brown: It's time for Big Tech to make some big changes**](https://click.mlsend.com/link/c/YT0xODc3Nzk1OTA3NTU1MDQwMDEzJmM9ZDZyMiZlPTAmYj04OTQ1MjY4OTImZD1kNHQxeDRs.E4ZmSaz7_6G4U5rL3lBPLBUeTXGmm3ahtCo5jna1E_M) The Boston Herald 02/02/2022 As Republicans, we have come to expect a double standard from the media. President George W. Bush’s daughters were often in the tabloids for being “party girls.” D-list entertainer Kathy Griffin called my teenage daughters “prostitutes” on national television, and Sarah Palin endured a fair amount of cruel ridicule herself, far too often directed at her daughters and other family members. Of course, you didn’t see President Clinton or President Obama’s daughters treated with such callous cruelty. Scott and I were fortunate, if that’s even the word to use, social media was still new when he was a Massachusetts senator. Instead of sharing news stories, people were beginning to make a transition from Myspace to Facebook, abandoning Tom Anderson for Mark Zuckerberg. Twitter was barely four years old, people were just discovering its usefulness and the art of crafting a message in less than 140 characters. Now, in 2022, the double standard that Republicans have come to expect over the years has taken a more nefarious turn. These days, it goes beyond mean-spirited comments on CNN to easily shared memes, fake quotes or stories that go “viral,” and worst of all, complete censorship of dissenting opinions...

Eugene, Oregon -- Doyle Canning filed to run for Congress today in Oregon's newly redrawn and Democratic leaning Fourth District. Canning, 42, of Eugene, is an environmentalist, attorney, and 20 year veteran community organizer. She is an alumna of the University of Oregon School of Law and was elected Vice-Chair of the Democratic Party of Oregon's Environmental Caucus in 2021. This is her second run for Congress, and she enters the race with over 200 donors and endorsements across the District. "Finally, a candidate who understands that we are in crisis and there is no more time to argue about whether global warming is real. It is here. We need experienced representatives like Doyle Canning in Congress, who understand that if we don't act now, we will have no future," said Casey MacKenzie of Port Orford, who chairs [Indivisible Curry County](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDPAZiEt8wwS6hp5wOIb3CiIEzoyKW-YGQLKSqDHYuA4XrYGp8_-F0dNZaSbn7ffsXgnRz4Vs_dwpHhp6kBKpjS5l9TlvwuQxkdy_bNUOE4I8be1D0f-cF0A1y8Wwo549mLPxeBzVRYZf8FIcZBkmDSWwmwssa5cysKw1Nqp6jiUXy93Bxul-26liAaWNRi56BAu_vCtedJLIqL7qHs5KDzIVhJ4PDlZjFNBEqfdVTSLhnfyZRux4BByRCXEou36n0izTi1fyfBwfN4SWLn5xSBq5rKtcEmoQOgxpmnvV9KIX/3j9/TqXLHGxXT4mEPAxQUSncPg/h2/obTPYuyybQhpaO0MF3TvVars6lvNp-kjhbgfbQt3a8c), which has endorsed Canning's campaign for Congress today. "Doyle Canning's priority is our kids. That's why we need her as our Member of Congress," said Eugene 4J School Board Member Maya Rabasa, who was an early backer of Canning's bid. "In a crisis, our community knows we can count on Canning to show up and lead. During the pandemic she has been an outspoken champion for our teachers, and is always seeking racial equity solutions for our schools. She's strategic, passionate, and progressive and I am confident that she will deliver change for our community in Congress." Corvallis City Councilor Charles Maughan is also supporting Canning: "I am focused on finding solutions to our homelessness crisis in Corvallis, and I know that when we elect Doyle Canning to Congress, she will work hard to push forward legislation that will address the needs of all Oregonians. We cannot wait for a livable climate, Medicare for All, fair wages and affordable housing any longer. She is the right person and the right time; we need to elect a progressive champion now and that's Doyle Canning." "Doyle Canning is the candidate in this race that I trust to make real progress on climate action. She has the courage, grit, and the knowhow we need to get things done in a new Congress," said Lincoln County Democratic Party Chair Micheal Gaskill. "Canning has a powerful vision for a blue economy for the Oregon coast - supporting tidal and wind energy and thriving fisheries - and the credibility to follow through. She'll make us all proud and we're ready to build a grassroots movement that delivers the win on May 17, and November 8." Canning's first campaign appearance will be at Friday's student climate strike, sponsored by Sunrise Movement Eugene. This event begins at South Eugene High School (400 E 19th Ave) at 12:00 PM with a colorful march to Washburne Park (21st & Agate), where Canning will join the rally for climate action and speak about her candidacy for Congress. (Masks are required). Background - Doyle Canning is running in the Democratic primary for Congress in the newly redrawn and Democratic leaning Fourth Congressional District of Oregon. The District has shifted to now include Lincoln County, and is rated [D+9 by 538](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/1XQbiUZqIgkFRQxQopJGKYk2OKRlV3DjCjdYjv9hTcXyYxQ_mn27vmTb6y4Zk5dQP531PvlcfX3cSysLQKtNj0owyJvNIhuNsBIOhopgn8nqMxPHQvMEGfB8DBcAhmVpyct7Ua4H7_f58lVExtNv6qxZh_i1iu3hsMgfrZU9pE5bc7mTBXqyaVKyvFaN3QhB9fuw31JSZMavmVFZ_53Fyq6IGLKkzSVkMgQtdB3L1p3sWeQKMd2eE_4vn64tTreZiluaxnVf1ThESLCiTNtKRN1eoStW6b2dkDzQuCup9y3i9Fe35yFKN0AH4ccVOtsKk_IZAFmXmIXxCroCeHum3Q/3j9/TqXLHGxXT4mEPAxQUSncPg/h3/AJFdhD5UZu8-E_I1bwdAxONvrPskm6mooe8evVgyC04), meaning it is a reliable Democratic stronghold in a year when Republican gains are expected elsewhere. Canning is committed to taking no campaign funds from the fossil fuel industry or corporate PACs, and legislating for bold climate action, a Medicare for All system that guarantees healthcare to all Americans, ending poverty, and advancing the rights of workers. She would be the first woman to represent Oregon's Fourth District in Congress. The primary election is May 17. Learn more about the Doyle Canning campaign at [canningforcongress.com](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/Esi6nwzs8Exib0g1JWeL813Lewzmyu-u1YRTpcm8FkLXUJJagpdTJGfQaDW6cSK7spqvc5ZOEJIcWv6d6ZcQ97Lzd3PlJI4FhBipfxP1nKxQsfRBp5tZgrGfDX3Bpl4KNKjb6WRZ3CF7SXCMdwZ3wheECyVT0Sy4RZ73Lz8KvEfImhU6EbXdrlkRsBFiU8j3HBrdJXZhdhJce1ZrqHQM9r6onh2i5Vb4f1PNhCNvWRtx7I2PDMMUF3cEDfpfL16F2y7mKWuTHZKgNj8FmwvI0Q/3j9/TqXLHGxXT4mEPAxQUSncPg/h4/WO-z_n4wFww3BKXWDgU_exuNQvvjdzRTdMbjW9unsDk).

She would be the first woman to represent Oregon's Fourth District in Congress. The primary election is May 17. Learn more about the Doyle Canning campaign at . | Thank you for your support, The Canning For Congress Team P.S. Contributions to Canning for Congress are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. To contribute via check, please address to "Canning for Congress" P.O. name: GOP Polling
subject: Q: Revive Iran deal?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
|

**BIDEN'S BRINGING BACK THE BAD:
[Don't trust the Iran deal to keep us safe »](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=51e3f5e750964123b7fcd9588138893f&_e=L-8C1oM_6GAHRJmA8jiO1AFr81aSG5qb1aGBqgDbWqXNGdk8IGhha9wWC7saPKP0dkNlGikzDavTV6UeIRtWtjuJjawDAZ9RT6vEiu9T_R6NCYfuyeC6SzdXW_s-Qq9LTw4zcbPgHp628ypA4ISAKhUL1B8y_Qv06MpIQiv6jH5X-turE1lp7ugYDitLTx5i2F4y9Z7-pBDLJMcMxScHlru5VdAMiwj8XkGQ5dFw1IOery4zvbUyHh4T8oGxcKna5jSGiiUjo2roP7r2B4vgao3sChIYNikT32xVnaHnWMzneGJp2Yo4vweAWpeD280QK_pHuT-CdesqVquFOnTVlOEAcwx_6vuOZXVapTjEBUNpvv_dsDp_9TOkS_qpgCGlKcO62A9yvVuOlXwhPpZlT3qa54NxcTHvBjI-7we5ytgdg5YK8wPjhEOQssVCoC7B) **
---
Fellow American,

President 45 knew the deal with Iran was bad news. That's why he ended it.

Now, Biden's all over the headlines working to "quickly" get it back in place. Even now, Iran is dictating the terms indirectly through mediators...

That is not what a strong America does.

So, Fellow American, [**will you follow this secure link and demand an end to Bumbling Biden's begging?**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=51e3f5e750964123b7fcd9588138893f&_e=L-8C1oM_6GAHRJmA8jiO1AFr81aSG5qb1aGBqgDbWqXNGdk8IGhha9wWC7saPKP0dkNlGikzDavTV6UeIRtWtjuJjawDAZ9RT6vEiu9T_R6NCYfuyeC6SzdXW_s-Qq9LTw4zcbPgHp628ypA4ISAKhUL1B8y_Qv06MpIQiv6jH5X-turE1lp7ugYDitLTx5i2F4y9Z7-pBDLJMcMxScHlru5VdAMiwj8XkGQ5dFw1IM7eQWthQqAZWtninDa4FrQ2e1WHrijLA3jILeDYU6QDLDE4qy9NDPp5f9DTWa_FK8gu58zZCWCifGfN34Odm146HlsCfBo7a_vY8HF406wUVGDeNvTHB2OX2qP6pRRJcyq3cBDcXlFDg6Z3qi7LTW24CgaiifC8v5y-DlPEDAlxbn25BM6OiBdxOCMJ2-Cfj2VRnFc--nrSg5s7QYLy2hd) Iran is in the driver's seat, and that is never safe for America.

Awaiting your response,
\- Senate Republicans

name: =?utf-8?Q?Nuestro=20PAC?=
subject: How We’re Helping Bring Change to Texas
body:
Even in a winter storm, Republicans don’t care about Texas | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/nuestropac/how-were-helping-bring-change-to-texas?e=b8f90417f9)
---
| |
---
| | -

Texas Republicans are too busy [banning books from libraries](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=5b101bf8a3&e=b8f90417f9), suppressing voters by [making it harder to vote by mail](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=37179c26ad&e=b8f90417f9), and [denying women the right to healthcare](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=3b8f75ffc5&e=b8f90417f9), that they haven’t thought about protecting [Texans from power outages during the upcoming winter storm.](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=1ab582721c&e=b8f90417f9)

Every single Texas U.S. Senator and U.S. House Representative voted against President Biden’s infrastructure bill that will bring in [$35 billion to Texas](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=c0936414ca&e=b8f90417f9) to fix the failing infrastructure Republicans have ignored for years. The working people of Texas should not have to worry about their pipes bursting, freezing in their homes, or losing their lives because Governor Greg Abbott and his Republican colleagues [prefer to cash in on money from major oil executives.](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=a9a22386e3&e=b8f90417f9)

Time is up for Texas Republicans who have financially and politically thrived on the misfortune of Texans and Texas’ poor, outdated infrastructure.

Nuestro Texas is committed to running multi-layered, culturally competent, and paid bilingual communication strategies that target Latinos. Just this week, [we announced our support for Jessica Cisneros](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=553fc20d6d&e=b8f90417f9), candidate for TX-28, by sending out 50,000 pieces of mail to Latinas in the district. Your support makes this programming possible.

Will you continue to join us in fighting to elect Democrats who will care for and deliver to the people of Texas? If so, [please consider making a contribution to Nuestro Texas.](https://solidaritystrategies.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=985fddf6f660c0281e297f819&id=d4be99db3c&e=b8f90417f9)

---
| As always, thank you for your efforts in helping Nuestro Texas educate, mobilize, and turn out Latinos.

\- Nuestro Texas

clean_body: I think you missed it. Friend, Did you see my email? I'm giving YOU the chance to INCREASE your IMPACT by 600%. Thank you, Donald J. Trump 45th President of the United States \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Donald J. Trump Date: January 31, 2021 To: Friend, I emailed you. My son, Don, emailed you. Kimberly Guilfoyle emailed you. Team Trump emailed you. And now I 'm emailing you, again. We have our FIRST FUNDRAISING DEADLINE OF THE YEAR coming up, and each day when I ask my team who has stepped up, they've never mentioned your name. Why is that, Friend? You've always been a strong ally in the past and this just isn't like you. The 2022 Election is THIS YEAR and your support is needed now more than ever. This offer expires at midnight tonight, Friend. This is your last chance, Friend. Next time I ask my team for an update on who contributed towards our FIRST End-of-Month goal, I want to hear your name. Thank you, Donald J. Trump 45th President of the United States Paid for by Save America JFC, a joint fundraising committee of Save America and Make America Great Again PAC. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Donaldjtrump.com Save America, PO Box 13570, Arlington, VA 22219 |

body:
NH Dems have a different agenda....
Dear Friend,
[New Hampshire Democrats sponsored Bills HB1113 and SB235 IGNORE SCIENCE and most importantly Granite State parents and students. A study done by John Hopkins University proves what we have been saying all along!](https://5wbjs096.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Fsecure.winred.com%2Fnh-gop%2Fparents-over-politicians/1/0100017ec1a25a37-267b4028-f01c-479b-a249-375a4b1c90ea-000000/nBNPx7YJJq_oD6AHBrz3FYxvSv0=256)

Bills HB1113 and SB235 are a sole pursuit of Democrats in lock step with the school boards who put the needs of teachers unions before New Hampshire students.

name: Tobias Read
subject: We can do better
body:
Good intentions without action are just empty promises.
---
| | Dear Peter,

Time and again we’ve seen Oregon flounder with pandemic response. First, we learned today that [**the state is charging qualified teachers $400 out of pocket to renew their licenses to substitute teach**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41405933/328691400/-2033765914?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMDExMWQwZS02ODg1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KV9o3Y_Yi9RWe1H1jN8rM0ZZZ0uwhUGV0cxEKSkOCTg=&emci=c4d3bbe7-6785-ec11-94f6-281878b85110&emdi=a0111d0e-6885-ec11-94f6-281878b85110&ceid=30662126) while school districts reimburse costs for non-certified emergency subs. Read that again. **Less qualified emergency subs don’t have to pay while teachers who have been certified in the past still have to pay up.** Don’t get me wrong – we need all Oregonians to step up and help our schools. But it’s this lack of planning, urgency, and leadership that is holding us back. If we are serious about keeping kids in school learning, then we need to prioritize getting more support to teachers who are facing burnout and frustration.

Then we learned that [**the state of Oregon is sitting on millions of at-home COVID tests with no clear plan to get them out.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41405934/328691401/-1010166084?eType=EmailBlastContent&eId=e7f3c8ac-7882-47e2-9f55-e3bab824a79d&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMDExMWQwZS02ODg1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KV9o3Y_Yi9RWe1H1jN8rM0ZZZ0uwhUGV0cxEKSkOCTg=&emci=c4d3bbe7-6785-ec11-94f6-281878b85110&emdi=a0111d0e-6885-ec11-94f6-281878b85110&ceid=30662126) Instead of being proactive, we’re waiting for organizations to request them. In nearby Washington, individual households can simply request them. In other states every town, village, and city is receiving tests to distribute to residents and organizations in their communities.

**But not in Oregon.**

This is now the third school year in which our kids are being impacted by Covid19 and our public health challenges. Our leaders need to act with urgency to help keep our kids safe and in school. **My opponent Tina Kotek resigned as Speaker of the House and left behind one crisis after another for others to fix.**

That’s not leadership. Good intentions without action are just empty promises. We need substitute teachers available immediately with no out of pocket costs to the people who step up, and the people of Oregon need Covid tests NOW, not tomorrow.

Oregon needs a Governor who sees the problems and steps up to solve them. Simply put... Oregon needs a change in approach. With your support, I’ll be that Governor.
Stay safe,

---

---

* * *
| [DONATE »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41405935/328691402/-2045037038?refcode=footer&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMDExMWQwZS02ODg1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KV9o3Y_Yi9RWe1H1jN8rM0ZZZ0uwhUGV0cxEKSkOCTg=)

subject: Revive Iran?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
|

**BIDEN'S BRINGING BACK THE BAD:
[Don't trust the Iran deal to keep us safe »](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=5c4303f8750546cd8280698138a22188&_e=w7kQ5PB-Dt6HtLvchgr0xN7DakqanQdI4cz9R4JAcKXpyknxrdocG4IeQCO-U13qJ5h1kwCNr1XqQd2biosV4YnHRD7I0QjAbqON5I9iGzvhpPimO5KMEKuZ6yo_hpJk6VuDqofVcoxa4eUNE-KN5wgOpuY8IASLJX8ZeuN5FfXGfTMRbGlxDXTkw0g5q6baRRsngMzexI7K4DaAwVR-OprnLs-QkywzY3HFjeL-JDed7sz0UCF-QHWLq8lCHbRHKA8ecQT8nAdExAqhYXf2AtLA--m-CQouLRtHqTCMKS3XIHzdBKSkjTX2h4HHtuerWtGUjwt-vT036XJOoUmjENFI9y8SOPYfveeoEOmnqQSP88sC3WBEyAJDm9hCz7kOF8qjRuYN7Hf-I5sMyma-ZNZI1dL-Nfa8zHqGzmHgE27zWIAn29vUoTtz80lR6HOIiaKYh4I8m8EtdLwjmnrWhw%3D%3D) **

Fellow American,

President 45 knew the deal with Iran was bad news. That's why he ended it.

Now, Biden's all over the headlines working to "quickly" get it back in place. Even now, Iran is dictating the terms indirectly through mediators...

That is not what a strong America does.

So, Fellow American, [**will you follow this secure link and demand an end to Bumbling Biden's begging?**](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=5c4303f8750546cd8280698138a22188&_e=w7kQ5PB-Dt6HtLvchgr0xN7DakqanQdI4cz9R4JAcKXpyknxrdocG4IeQCO-U13qJ5h1kwCNr1XqQd2biosV4YnHRD7I0QjAbqON5I9iGzvhpPimO5KMEKuZ6yo_hpJk6VuDqofVcoxa4eUNE-KN5wgOpuY8IASLJX8ZeuN5FfXGfTMRbGlxDXTkw0g5q6baRRsngMzexI7K4DaAwVR-OprnLs-QkywzY3HFjeL-JDewcu7Q2cimCm7TR-s2EvNcmwBIrUxK9h01YcwCbO6UdJ4E25qIcr-0rktBfh0x4RNFHfojyx4FBmxkJ4AMXyekljrqQfxdYCSSxi3PDeNmVrM_M3QsKzhbKUQB7cmfP0RpS5SoeYvHeIwdR535wBZ_KKiejbNiNw1xAkO4Zt-QGJRJJIkIir77OZ596wxjJb3uAw8aqvMuONXpGYJLqDp7ydcQfJnwF9JOCtaFGPyw2Q%3D%3D) Iran is in the driver's seat, and that is never safe for America.

Team, January was an incredible month, and because of you, we started the year off on an impressive note. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41402015/328651448/-652894707?refcode=EM_CT_220203TY_CT_TC_CC_4_G_N_P1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTM5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZjJkOGZmMi00MTg1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kg_2F8KwqdAKQtCrlHOx469gzhKLHG-NXkWqUqj30tg=) I wanted to come in and say thank you. Every month of this campaign has left me more amazed than the last, and everything that we’re accomplishing has been because of each of you. **I am so grateful to have you along on this journey.** I know that this road will not be easy, but nothing good ever is. Protecting our democracy and ensuring that every voter has the opportunity to share their voice makes every trial worth it, and together, we will triumph. With immense gratitude, Chelsea | | | Democrat Chelsea Clark is a small business owner, a city councilwoman, a sixth-generation farmer, and a single mom. She's running to be Ohio's Secretary of State because the constitutional right to vote is under attack. Chelsea will fight to make sure that we all have access to vote, that all votes are counted, and that our elections are safe and secure.

name: Pat Fallon
subject: Peter, I will never apologize...
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FALLON FOR CONGRESS]( "PAID FOR BY FALLON FOR CONGRESS")
body:
to the Radical Progressives and RINOs.

| | | | | [](http://tracking.fallon4texas.com/f/a/hbXoTYu5FDouSeyaAmT1Ow~~/AAAHUQA~/RgRj3uCFP0UgODQ3MmI4MDIwMDk3MGY0ZmQ2ZTYzNTQ5NDAzNzRiYmVEQmh0dHBzOi8vc2VjdXJlLndpbnJlZC5jb20vZmFsbG9uLWZvci1jb25ncmVzcy9uZXZlcmFwb2xvZ2l6ZS1jYXRjaFcFc3BjZXVCCmH6hVv8YeYbhAFSFGRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tWAQAABgX)
---
| | Peter,

The Radical Left and weak RINOs both want to defeat me. **The RINOs have even adopted the left's talking points, declaring war on American success and the American Dream itself.**

Why do they hate me so much? It's because **I refuse to join them in apologizing for things they either should be proud of or had nothing to do with**. Allow me to be clear...

**I WILL NEVER APOLOGIZE FOR:**

* Being a Christian
* Loving my country
* Opposing further lockdowns
* Standing for the Flag
* Securing our border
* Fighting vaccine mandates
* Kneeling at the Cross
* Respecting/Admiring Law Enforcement
* Believing ALL LIVES MATTER
* Supporting harsh penalties for predators who harm the innocent
* Being a Capitalist
* Protecting the sanctity of Life
* Banning CRT in our schools
* Having worn our Country’s Uniform
* Safeguarding the 2nd Amendment
* Defending Religious Liberty
* Telling the truth no matter how ‘inconvenient’ it is to the Left’s narrative
* Cutting taxes
* Promoting personal responsibility
* Battling the socialist mob
* Exposing Left/Media’s blatant hypocrisy
* Championing our Military
* Calling Antifa domestic terrorists
* Celebrating American Exceptionalism
* Cherishing the United States of America and knowing that we have been blessed by the Hand of God and are the most exceptional nation history has ever known!

God bless America and may he keep Texas Great!

**Pat Fallon**
Conservative Congressman TX-04

name: Max Steiner
subject: Come say hello next week!
body:
The more I can count on grassroots supporters like you, the more time I can spend meeting with voters on the issues. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41402376/328652950/390307162?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTYyOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZWYzZThjZC00NDg1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=iT3kHGksQxNT16t7UwTi4rMA_4UV3zepafu7WET72Fg=)
Friend,

I wanted to take a moment to thank you for your ongoing support of my campaign. With your support, we continue to prove our strength in this race and we're sending a powerful message to Doug LaMalfa that his days in Congress are numbered.

This month we begin the process of getting my name officially listed on the ballot in CA-01. That means we're hard at work talking to as many voters as possible, gathering signatures, and elevating the issues that deserve our urgent attention -- from our droughts to wildfires, and the needs of small businesses and working families.

**One way we do this is by tabling at local hotspots and giving people the opportunity to talk to me directly.**
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41402377/328652951/360059203?refcode=em-2.3.22-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTYyOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZWYzZThjZC00NDg1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=iT3kHGksQxNT16t7UwTi4rMA_4UV3zepafu7WET72Fg=)
_Tabling in Redding last week_
I want to stress that none of our work would be possible with your support. The more I can count on grassroots supporters like you, the more time I can spend meeting with voters on the issues.

Next Wednesday, I'll be holding "office hours'' at Daycamp Coffee in Chico. If you're in the area, I'd love for you to stop by and say hello. If you can make it, just reply to the email and let me know you'll be there.
**Meet Max Steiner**
Wednesday February 9th between 7:00 am - 4:00pm
Daycamp Coffee 1925 Market Pl STE 150
Chico, CA 95928
_Reply to this email to RSVP_

Thanks again, friend.

Max | **[Donate »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41402378/328652952/360059203?refcode=em-2.3.22-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTYyOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZWYzZThjZC00NDg1LWVjMTEtOTRmNi0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=iT3kHGksQxNT16t7UwTi4rMA_4UV3zepafu7WET72Fg=)**

One way we do this is by tabling at local hotspots and giving people the opportunity to talk to me directly. = Tabling in Redding last week I want to stress that none of our work would be possible with your support. The more I can count on grassroots supporters like you, the more time I can spend meeting with voters on the issues. Next Wednesday, I'll be holding "office hours'' at Daycamp Coffee in Chico. If you're in the area, I'd love for you to stop by and say hello. If you can make it, just reply to the email and let me know you'll be there. Meet Max Steiner Wednesday February 9th between 7:00 am - 4:00pm Daycamp Coffee 1925 Market Pl STE 150 Chico, CA 95928 Reply to this email to RSVP Thanks again, friend. Max | = | = Max Steiner is a combat veteran, foreign service officer, and Democrat running to flip California's 1st District from red to blue. Consider supporting Max's campaign by = If you'd like to receive less email, add your name We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Max Steiner is a combat veteran of the Iraq War and Sergeant First Class in the Army Reserves. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense._ Max Steiner for Congress 2485 Notre Dame Blvd, Ste 370 #194 Chico, CA 95928 United States

body:
|

**BIDEN'S BRINGING BACK THE BAD:
[Don't trust the Iran deal to keep us safe »](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=f55bd64c24904fd89df699b6f5df1c5d&_e=crcaqR0p21l5QH1yV16ja2GsUa0cGb2LSwLVJEEXpT2kd_XC8hbR8jBrghsZ7u-YS7ULPYpOHcrF9ABh1DHv4SiB9dG_1YpsYZAEafSFgFjArGQw59chs9daqjetHT2YLeJo5pOuDQlHq09Sn2mZ3_uHWhqP3a_ASh9QIJKVJ2PC0aTKLfZdfASOOfkI-iRYgcmjkJa3x-lyjGxjUg1HaVbGf4lY472uJ7pTsF3zYVTVMhy8ARTajTTtJ2oGPD1J3Z7h1BFPvIDhjyNsyXsEasJxjKkfiul4qH1VhdA8Y4HHz7tobI4x2pc4p0OucXpzkfUp2KU_AKrzDTcJipEUjIjLldspmoFJ-VUDCS44B6r5sVgSr93LPdVsqZMt4XZRwLCmApel4FrkUM0CzvJxT_ggsOnfBhdFWmQHFgGPtO9j8iWYc13INOKdamWVdpL-CB5fQamAhqdh2kasbXbD2jUpxw8U_p1KyQ533gVbWJ4%3D) **
---
Peter,

President 45 knew the deal with Iran was bad news. That's why he ended it.

Now, Biden's all over the headlines working to "quickly" get it back in place. Even now, Iran is dictating the terms indirectly through mediators...

That is not what a strong America does.

So, Peter, [**will you follow this secure link and demand an end to Bumbling Biden's begging?**](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=f55bd64c24904fd89df699b6f5df1c5d&_e=crcaqR0p21l5QH1yV16ja2GsUa0cGb2LSwLVJEEXpT2kd_XC8hbR8jBrghsZ7u-YS7ULPYpOHcrF9ABh1DHv4SiB9dG_1YpsYZAEafSFgFjArGQw59chs9daqjetHT2YLeJo5pOuDQlHq09Sn2mZ3_uHWhqP3a_ASh9QIJKVJ2PC0aTKLfZdfASOOfkI-iRYgcmjkJa3x-lyjGxjUg1HaVbGf4lY472uJ7pTsF3zYVTVMhy8ARTajTTtJ2oGPD1JlhVmtyNSighiWzSaVfOBDO8gLMvk0vH3CD7M_sxrkoMTKPTCZiPWKP76jxMYkOHv7-0xwqxXb6jpvErP1i_F9aNpg6c4LZEUOlOpBnyv8aO_QSRMsjtvkTfCC_3oxHofxUFae9G5Qf4Ce7Dm0LrmIRmF9EyFByrIoCmZA6-LZHznC060g2MZBA8mu0twQc3HEHDuxotRuOjS9cuDHU3s24NSUGzLvIBGFmggX16e-CY%3D) Iran is in the driver's seat, and that is never safe for America.

Awaiting your response,
\- Senate Republicans

name: Ian Smith
subject: They just sentenced me...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Murphy's Favorite Gym
body:
Because I chose to stand for freedom!
---
| | | | | | | | Friend,
---
|
**The State of New Jersey has sentenced me to ONE YEAR of probation because I chose to take a stand. ** I didn't back down, I didn't give in, and I never surrendered to the Left's tyranny when they demanded I shut my small business down.

New Jersey's power hungry Governor and his army of corrupt, big-government bureaucrats went on a warpath to make my life hell _**because I didn 't want to comply with non-scientific, authoritarian, unconstitutional mandates...**_

name: Sara Jacobs
subject: why uplifting Black history matters
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
Let’s all recommit ourselves to creating a fairer, more just future for us all.

| This week marks the start of Black History Month, a time to celebrate the incredible history and achievements of Black Americans.

Black history is American history. At this moment when there is so much effort to distort and deny the past, it is essential that we continue to celebrate the incredible achievements Black Americans have made throughout our history in the face of hatred and systemic oppression.

This hatred and systemic racism still persist, as Black Americans continue to face discrimination in housing, employment, the criminal justice system, voter suppression efforts, and more.

Let’s all recommit ourselves to creating a fairer, more just future for us all. I look forward to working alongside you as we do.

In solidarity,
Sara

P.S. Below are some of my favorite books by Black authors that I hope you’ll read:

clean_body: Let’s all recommit ourselves to creating a fairer, more just future for us all. | This week marks the start of Black History Month, a time to celebrate the incredible history and achievements of Black Americans. Black history is American history. At this moment when there is so much effort to distort and deny the past, it is essential that we continue to celebrate the incredible achievements Black Americans have made throughout our history in the face of hatred and systemic oppression. This hatred and systemic racism still persist, as Black Americans continue to face discrimination in housing, employment, the criminal justice system, voter suppression efforts, and more. Let’s all recommit ourselves to creating a fairer, more just future for us all. I look forward to working alongside you as we do. In solidarity, Sara P.S. Below are some of my favorite books by Black authors that I hope you’ll read: * | | = Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress Sara Jacobs for Congress PO Box 120085 San Diego, CA 92112 United States | | | ---|---|--- If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please

name: =?utf-8?Q?Nathan=20Hochman=20for=20Attorney=20General=202022?=
subject: Victim shaming? Really?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nathan Hochman for Attorney General 2022
body:
| | |
---
| | Victim shaming?

In the aftermath of the nationwide attention brought to California when Union Pacific trains were looted and robbed by criminals in Los Angeles, LA District Attorney George Gascón's response was to shame the victim. He claimed that it was Union Pacific's fault for not providing enough security for the trains.

Really?

That's like saying a small business owner that is robbed is responsible because all of their products aren't under lock and key.

The real problem here is that criminals who engaged in the robberies were released within 24 hours of being apprehended, with zero bail.
---
| [ ](https://strategyinsightshq.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=634ddc357d96a36fb118fa25c&id=65965598c7&e=94837ebab3 "#WTF — Let 'Em Go Guy George Gascon and Victim Shaming #shorts")
---
| It's clear that prosecutors like The Let ‘Em Go Guys (LA DA George Gascón, SF DA Chesa Boudin, and AG Rob Bonta) would rather victim shame than address the root causes of increased crime: **_their own failures._**

If The Let 'Em Go Guys don’t want to do their jobs, then we need to let them go and bring in someone who will.
---
|
---
| If I have the honor of being elected Attorney General, I will never blame the victim. I will enact policies that prevent crime from happening in the first place.

Sincerely,

Nathan Hochman
_Candidate for California Attorney General_
---
| |
---

name: Jack Ballard
subject: MT & Black history
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JACK BALLARD FOR MONTANA
body:
Hi all, Today marks three days into Black History Month. Please join me in celebrating the many accomplishments and contributions that Black American | | | [](http://links.ballardformontana.com/u/click?_t=01481013522b4e28a72d84f828a757ef&_m=ade6a45f5cbc4094a7d5687f10e986cf&_e=zG5h-9b_GTg2JVE_3dSCJ64M-OEl7t9yvBn0HTvG4pEynJYUCqS1Gtt_0KX-DlQ5BV6Q1F8hSvqXkTFWwjQuO79ZH1iHSjjeLs0c4a8LlPhDx34ZO9dGSa80WndxhNaLam34hlgb0ybQqQVr6Pd401Vra7yUKhg7YD_7pbOB4AjuG33RW-xx0rbsOeAwfWEprct3rvSrVaqJmYpRXn_HpcLixi0wHVQXKwtnVhFPH60mUfKWxlQ1a_0kWxDRN5S9lFhsFuvX2IkGzwBQKqfbw35HxAtNfCEu8Gh-QQSXGgkBzpKWzDSqwhmz15AbFuoIGCHCej6Pqy_Xf4PcQqZiSQXl8Txut_-UtyIQgUj1eRhMYmK5BeB4d6SUZxvz_gAR9uzTml-AJiiWFcyVjtQWLTLi6e2YIHbiH4_R5a29Sh_3Pkz0p2ifDEFBjg8T6E-hxd9BwXaMqRj9zX3yBckKDt6kaucc_Ywqr8W9Phwtnegs2gBOF5azBJqoCFLetjMn) Hi all, Today marks three days into Black History Month. Please join me in celebrating the many accomplishments and contributions that Black Americans have made to all aspects of our country. Black History Month was created nearly 100 years ago in 1926 to honor the countless contributions African Americans have made to the United States despite generations of slavery, Jim Crow discrimination, and other forms of oppression – some of which still survive today. It’s essential that we take time this month to honor, celebrate, and continue our education on Black history in America and how it has shaped our society. Right here in Montana, Black Americans have been essential to the formation of our society. I encourage you to take a look at this resource provided by the Montana Historical Society about Montana’s Black history: [https://mhs.mt.gov/Shpo/AfricanAmericans/History/Timeline](http://links.ballardformontana.com/u/click?_t=01481013522b4e28a72d84f828a757ef&_m=ade6a45f5cbc4094a7d5687f10e986cf&_e=zG5h-9b_GTg2JVE_3dSCJ6hfmg4PvFp8EAo8kH5t84eJvc3rmTMwP44befEuwbullK_hR5OdZ5mbohBwkh523YUe38xV706jhQAhyGFr2LEa97HsQcdEMl_6SEI41SY7evKTrKOs3A-7y_2lRcM6By_tc3qCBNLKyS52QaiITuEVyGh1sD8P4LdIN48SFLfYLws3RrZt9Pz82LqByE5P26zOR1_bUkQB4vNjFrZpVwLGVTb0ckvS0YdmQAOliE3WVtgIXVBs1HXhB81-YbMgQvPI_SoMZ8siKjBKk0A1pO3nuybwO-MThsjftmBmokDA)

Thank you,
Jack Jack Ballard grew up on a ranch west of Three Forks homesteaded by his grandfather in 1907. He knows how to fight for Montana and he intends to fight for you and MT-02 in Washington D.C. **[CONTRIBUTE MONTHLY](http://links.ballardformontana.com/u/click?_t=01481013522b4e28a72d84f828a757ef&_m=ade6a45f5cbc4094a7d5687f10e986cf&_e=zG5h-9b_GTg2JVE_3dSCJ64M-OEl7t9yvBn0HTvG4pEynJYUCqS1Gtt_0KX-DlQ5BV6Q1F8hSvqXkTFWwjQuO79ZH1iHSjjeLs0c4a8LlPhDx34ZO9dGSa80WndxhNaLam34hlgb0ybQqQVr6Pd401Vra7yUKhg7YD_7pbOB4AjuG33RW-xx0rbsOeAwfWEprct3rvSrVaqJmYpRXn_HpcLixi0wHVQXKwtnVhFPH60mUfKWxlQ1a_0kWxDRN5S9lFhsFuvX2IkGzwBQKqfbw89mkyn6VfVN5A1rvBoDF6c0kpX1jNIXjQHWvLXN4a2SJHNLNPJfZYDRR8SEa-wm2GT351iJQ-keY0vbTVhi8fUFn0-FkMCTnXANw-2gMMn1ZRyltO6_kxh0Beq57B_7sbOToLhzUhlhn-JqyuNw0rnJ08IeKi9-ckAAsMlJdQuciGa5Z4WV7lkKNSWL5SZy5Tp7_6hXS-sGGOtnEv10dhiLVobYzuEA8R68n1lxzTp5GqYEMPhklvO1DJsFp0n6BQ%3D%3D)**** ** PAID FOR BY JACK BALLARD FOR MONTANA.

Dear Friend,

The recent attacks against our brave men and women in law enforcement are alarming and disgusting.
This must stop – police officers deserve our respect, support, and strong backing.
---

Frankly, I’m shocked that there are political figures and activists who are still proudly advocating to defund the police.

These proposals are radical and dangerous — and they put our families in jeopardy.
We must stand with law enforcement to provide more resources and training so that they can protect our communities and put violent criminals behind bars.

[**Will you join me?**](https://u21639199.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=DRhu97MwZOafZz0axKBSuHM4TyUiRDjO7WRT-2Bn4igr193XMreDNS7fl2A4g9SL86i-2FQV5YOPuH0GDsJb-2FO7ExrtCsveAU0JWlli6xhdQPWnMS0DD-2FF6-2FCh9Dg-2FQfffWNu7TI_XwU9lPq5-2Bze3gI64ohlV0cQwWAuBAU7ADIvj2CS7ymhuTI2jG3IYR5NWbV52qgmdAiGhBklNQsRXTWl4rWtz6V26ifmsOhc2lTNkdXy-2F5FFomLfxGZOGa3bZcww1pfz6zBVtEJdVvuPoeu4elvvHbwaVpXXghXQYzMdN-2BEdRr-2F1R6mDXoebxWByNgFpR-2FND6-2FCWd19DdPmgOvqQp2VLVLyFCaGKKirJtS9epd9amMf5Yk-2BPiK5IaebNhLQG1xLZuEv0-2FGgTzLOah9OwFRnFxnDvN99H3lTOzjUresMruC8ySdDd43zru67ejIvAMLRMLC5Jvd0-2FAS3BN0Ldd-2FhaDP-2FerUnPlqQ02XHbVPKoGmOlwyO9sCY01DJSw2r1mJhK6iMNSyZykM04JdCE6witqhegEjJGtmn9J1U5Gr7XozYgaN2BgntA4nCKdOWggd8htFsZ-2BMyAvmRTilPLMDawEc-2F2KnR2pnsEQPIWaVGGaXkgSnJyKnO5PJXhjhPuyxNFQKdAN5G-2FeiWYwN6YCAfrQjLtdChuCIPsYuSVP8sUHBiZ0sXmdoiLptJvxzWTHJCz5RA6S3MATcmCXld-2FA2R-2FjRQ-2BamWGKr2Rk-2F8hNTMRJSZVmSeHon8hjwTGzYw-2B5FZCSbhEy5DP7v5iPMvS-2FN7PjGkCgaqbAl9rL8gRUw9eaflJjqlu4Fl43DpDTBxrOR809tMvxzLCiKY6y2i38SwWqpC0vi73OTsTNICs8xEUhirugVHwHAcMVCeBLEt20EBK3QmTF6oWktQrRFW2F-2BrY-2BPBvbYFrvP0Sx7GfLVusfMcEnZej8XVvKn-2FuTedkPbi-2B7t4qDV8ImJqWYEtaKZfL4KcaOkazA-2BQgIJ629iNY8NfFPSnCOQyOmqjqOO0b8PUMZSfzQsNBs-2Fi5o8xiEQn8RD8PokXZbLTt0MRnffmaX4fw6BHT9QdgVNBv5fLtGN7ph)

clean_body: | | | | Dear Friend, The recent attacks against our brave men and women in law enforcement are alarming and disgusting. This must stop – police officers deserve our respect, support, and strong backing. Frankly, I’m shocked that there are political figures and activists who are still proudly advocating to defund the police. These proposals are radical and dangerous — and they put our families in jeopardy. We must stand with law enforcement to provide more resources and training so that they can protect our communities and put violent criminals behind bars. Thank you, | | Follow Us! | "Facebook" | "Twitter" | instagram.com/kellymschulz "Instagram" ---|---|--- Kelly Schulz P.O. Box 6528, Annapolis, MD 21401 Authority: Kelly Schulz for Governor, Inc. Treasurer: Sam Malhotra

body:
|

**BIDEN'S BRINGING BACK THE BAD:
[Don't trust the Iran deal to keep us safe »](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=71abbd65216c4478a4015caf419a6d11&_e=aNKTrKjwc-Vb34FvLX-BKLWWe_b99_xlg12sJFjMKNH_vMNnAbxMASD9T-r1L8QkKbBCpH4DwDIXSMt0Abv_DwFB96-jUL7HGHyLddGhwW-TNPEjxH6lF1gdJWNaPmzNyfruhQ6bVqFLW1VNVvFzTMt2HU4vMP0KAeXU_trmy8w38OBUWOLLFkEaJgz80wS7vnwaXW66KfxuiU4In3R22TX66YW7PKq3q5mhl6V5M4kD5H_Ugh6y_cwYsZXBCcNEGrEF1cpBg5ptKzagW2GH9sc9ZptGB-GLyNFDD4c-Z69tG8ViYkwp5n3W6tVEY_kA1c-4IqQxG5QH9JwzIy2n8390L8Q94HdC_XdBbHerOLbEvOf8FldPOdSmyDoP6n3TAHKsTVnPVlcESGfcmfMoaSfKx2QoOsmfixwzJh9GS4tShN-1DMhd7jbIhOA4ZDx4PMfwk8v2OTB8mHEX5LFDRg%3D%3D) **
---
Fellow American,

President 45 knew the deal with Iran was bad news. That's why he ended it.

Now, Biden's all over the headlines working to "quickly" get it back in place. Even now, Iran is dictating the terms indirectly through mediators...

That is not what a strong America does.

So, Fellow American, [**will you follow this secure link and demand an end to Bumbling Biden's begging?**](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=71abbd65216c4478a4015caf419a6d11&_e=aNKTrKjwc-Vb34FvLX-BKLWWe_b99_xlg12sJFjMKNH_vMNnAbxMASD9T-r1L8QkKbBCpH4DwDIXSMt0Abv_DwFB96-jUL7HGHyLddGhwW-TNPEjxH6lF1gdJWNaPmzNyfruhQ6bVqFLW1VNVvFzTMt2HU4vMP0KAeXU_trmy8w38OBUWOLLFkEaJgz80wS7vnwaXW66KfxuiU4In3R22TX66YW7PKq3q5mhl6V5M4kD5H_Ugh6y_cwYsZXBCcNEm46vYA5l2oPWUws5FcmPu66-6w7hc__U0D9J9rZ_aLg6uk3DMGckci4NIDZRok8G_RrcfYKXUyKnRivodd60gBOhggFJj7sLPEq0NGR-mLs0dJvOZagzWgmB-eZv3sVuR6ju2WUbP-6LXLgZkmVFgSq714U5DbYO0-Q3I4ObFnGrsMAQPFuG1AWnQkdiOa6A74iMmctkQoyDdlJ3LFni1Q%3D%3D) Iran is in the driver's seat, and that is never safe for America.

Awaiting your response,
\- Senate Republicans

Peter,

I have been blessed with many amazing opportunities, but I can say without a doubt that running for the Senate in Georgia is one of the greatest honors of my life.

That's why we're giving this race 110% Every. Single. Day.

Having said that, we're running against the top Senate fundraiser in the country. In the last three months, he raised more than $9.8 million!

Since he began raising money for his re-election campaign last winter, **he's brought in more than $53.5 MILLION.**

While this is a tremendous amount of money, we've never been known to back down from a good challenge.

**[Team Herschel needs 340 conservatives to make a donation of at least $3 before our end-of-month deadline tonight at midnight.](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=192a6469a6f34cd4b0de0b655324bb2d&_e=4Zdfzc4EqYb8RFPTTyaaLu4lMeapf3NgWqyT7VMpwUhU-00cGUyISo_jUQ_IJyJs1P2aBqI9o0uEUytGw-N7snfWyjLc1VsCvx7JE8EnRg_bWGFu9lo5_WIbZTDybMHRMcKYqdPcOE4jFbbTwilS9HlH2WH2EtN9IsCr7qHDukIUOkAyFjICP2tvCmbe12YSw1AMJGRZIHWVtr6R-6WssxEUIbylD-M0nS5IHlkDXfQQBbWKl9H8v6-_pTZ1NtGAt_jK1wEWmYWx0bKEU8S88gtrvo8AB166E2t0I7FGowl4HQXfWUxrXaVmlLbqPbUz-CeyBYJhrdfUp3BMnT4vxV3dbvJGrmtoj5czBkYuo2ZhMQ2q9K4yCRLS6EirU30wQhiOZ5QKr-ymZ-WDJVkrWp9HzYN35AbQfjHRTeFpuv3xyczWI3ZU8CpMGXBT80xlqtoTJ2hYdj-fmbxQYjSTEyOaOxqvDeuLXW-5qdgchwY7VLL4mioVdGjF5jNWBbrz)**

While our opponent may have a fundraising lead - for now, we've got the support of the voters. But we need to narrow the fundraising gap.

Would you please help us knock this out one donation at a time? _Starting with your contribution of $3 before midnight?_

clean_body: My Democrat opponent has brought in more than $53.5 MILLION. My Democrat opponent has brought in more than $53.5 MILLION. | Peter, I have been blessed with many amazing opportunities, but I can say without a doubt that running for the Senate in Georgia is one of the greatest honors of my life. That's why we're giving this race 110% Every. Single. Day. Having said that, we're running against the top Senate fundraiser in the country. In the last three months, he raised more than $9.8 million! Since he began raising money for his re-election campaign last winter, he's brought in more than $53.5 MILLION. While this is a tremendous amount of money, we've never been known to back down from a good challenge. While our opponent may have a fundraising lead - for now, we've got the support of the voters. But we need to narrow the fundraising gap. Would you please help us knock this out one donation at a time? Starting with your contribution of $3 before midnight? Thank you, Herschel Walker | | PAID FOR BY TEAM HERSCHEL, INC. Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go, but ._

body:
[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/46?t=1001&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ][IMG]

Hi folks—just checking in to see if you saw the email I sent
yesterday.

Truth is, we still aren't on track to make up our January fundraising
deficit. So, I wanted to reach out personally and ask one more time:
[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/7067?t=1007&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ]Will you chip in ASAP to get our campaign back on track? We can't flip
Ohio blue without you.

[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/7068?t=1016&amount=28&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ]Donate Now

Thanks,

Tim

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Tim <info@e.timforoh.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 2, 2022 at 4:08 PM
Subject: this one stings

Team,

First and foremost, I want to thank you for being a part of this
campaign—I mean it when I say that we can’t win this thing without you.

But, folks, I’m coming to you with a bit of bad news: we came up short on
January’s fundraising goal.

I’m not going to lie, this one stings. Missing our first critical goal of
2022 really puts us in a vulnerable position heading into this
all-important election year.

[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/7067?t=1008&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ]If we’re going to overcome the GOP’s millions and stop Mitch McConnell,
we cannot afford to fall behind again. Can you help me out by chipping in
$28 before midnight to help us make up for this temporary lapse?

[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/7068?t=1017&amount=28&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ]Donate $28 [ https://act.timforoh.com/go/7068?t=1018&amount=50&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ]Donate $50
[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/7068?t=1019&amount=100&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ]Donate $100 [ https://act.timforoh.com/go/7068?t=1020&amount=200&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ]Donate $200
[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/7068?t=1021&amount=250&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ]Donate $250 [ https://act.timforoh.com/go/7068?t=1022&amount=25&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ]Other Amount

Republicans are pouring tens of millions of dollars into Ohio, trying to
buy this Senate seat so that they can keep lining their pockets. They
don’t give a damn about working families.

This race can go one of two ways: either we lose and hand over the Senate
majority to McConnell, or we flip this seat, expand our majority, and
finally pass some legislation.

That all depends on you, Team.

[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/7067?t=1009&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ]If we can make up the difference in the next 24 hours, we can erase
our deficit and get back on track. Can I count on you to step up and chip
in $28 now? I wouldn’t ask if it wasn’t important.

[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/7068?t=1023&amount=28&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ]Donate $28 [ https://act.timforoh.com/go/7068?t=1011&amount=50&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ]Donate $50
[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/7068?t=1012&amount=100&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ]Donate $100 [ https://act.timforoh.com/go/7068?t=1013&amount=200&akid=2239%2E3611125%2E0l-Bp6 ]Donate $200

Thanks,

Tim

name: Ally For Utah
subject: Black History Month, Clarity Roundtable, Signature Gathering and more!
body:
#AllyForUtah Update | The latest news from the Ally Isom For U.S. Senate Campaign
---
| | | | | | |
---
[ ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001UV80nF325Xl6gYvWr5PqaUIQyUVLPhNIzu7IvWR9bycZzQDDhsWJjS_1yWpvyYOOpP3b7mKLfH-nGPk4NmwecQkxL3KyVRGtAY5BVckjE2YpZMKgT3NHBdDGEuupJxkBrON6U4CQ-Fm6vivNHMmUjA==&c=I0nMU01PR6ekQjRFb6dBJvE1QYqpLe-IxT9QQnHHYUjvlvxVWLeIhA==&ch=pm-eKGb0hO5ls-Eqn8Zp6Im-kHoMRZyf-1_7HoEdpPuI1kpV3cd84A==)
---
| | Friend,
Ally Isom has engaged in several important conversations about Clarity and inclusiveness lately. She was involved in a Black History Month conversation with women leaders and a Clarity Roundtable discussion with communication experts. KPCW radio's Leslie Thatcher also interviewed Ally. Finally, find out where you can go to sign a petition to get Ally's name on the June 2022 Republican Primary ballot.
---
| [ ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001UV80nF325Xl6gYvWr5PqaUIQyUVLPhNIzu7IvWR9bycZzQDDhsWJjXPGeotP2JEneks7wxp8QirGPSzKL9qXcdZmxWjglxlSqwLrSULy0He0GFvRznH7T-rwwolIKBYSflKh0k_hNxu1EA3yGHGzNhAAx-XoVACdrMpaLXUtKQIyANrrEUOBGg==&c=I0nMU01PR6ekQjRFb6dBJvE1QYqpLe-IxT9QQnHHYUjvlvxVWLeIhA==&ch=pm-eKGb0hO5ls-Eqn8Zp6Im-kHoMRZyf-1_7HoEdpPuI1kpV3cd84A==)
---
| Black History Month Commemoration Leaders from Women Who Succeed invited Ally to be part of a recorded discussion about the dreams, challenges and opportunities for Black Utahns. This was held to commemorate Black History Month.
These conversations are important to Ally and one of the reasons she is running for the U.S. Senate. Ally deeply believes that we need approachable elected officials who actively engage with their constituents. The right things can only get done when all voices are at the table.
Watch the full event [here.](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001UV80nF325Xl6gYvWr5PqaUIQyUVLPhNIzu7IvWR9bycZzQDDhsWJjXBw1ggLsATSLDKECokTUBQFuEpNpxd-sDJrxd2Jx0niyVgvPAZmiiccno9_qib9FzsFf1vHmu86sPzKChhaxrD3nJN136kVERux6R6jg5wNn-Q3G2SSpTEMoo6-sfYPaw==&c=I0nMU01PR6ekQjRFb6dBJvE1QYqpLe-IxT9QQnHHYUjvlvxVWLeIhA==&ch=pm-eKGb0hO5ls-Eqn8Zp6Im-kHoMRZyf-1_7HoEdpPuI1kpV3cd84A==) Learn why listening matters to Ally [here](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001UV80nF325Xl6gYvWr5PqaUIQyUVLPhNIzu7IvWR9bycZzQDDhsWJjb9ltaXpPb9xkLKSVBjIHoABed8vDkIY6mrezDRFdT7_p8Fb-u9uPgNGgN0mPelV-glIn4f1J0SiTnXoTH8a9cU8ODiDpKAZT3PblRktGLVm&c=I0nMU01PR6ekQjRFb6dBJvE1QYqpLe-IxT9QQnHHYUjvlvxVWLeIhA==&ch=pm-eKGb0hO5ls-Eqn8Zp6Im-kHoMRZyf-1_7HoEdpPuI1kpV3cd84A==).
---
|
---
| KPCW Interview Ally chats with Leslie Thatcher from KPCW radio in Park City to discuss her campaign and explain why she entered the race against an incumbent.
"It's time for good people to get off the sidelines and get into the conversation and invite all people to the table. That's what I've done my whole career," said Ally. "I've tried to build opportunities for people to come together to solve really thorny problems. And it all begins by talking."
Listen to clips of the interview [here](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001UV80nF325Xl6gYvWr5PqaUIQyUVLPhNIzu7IvWR9bycZzQDDhsWJjXBw1ggLsATSF_LhHsuF2B6GqpjYawC-kbAzM2ISPJi5ZD3l8ahyVu6cX1a6N-u6SFB2biFWgZPHaMAMEUslfmQFLtmGOXTulDwBs1fj0Ks6h2py4qDCuSOFmRhk5UfYWA==&c=I0nMU01PR6ekQjRFb6dBJvE1QYqpLe-IxT9QQnHHYUjvlvxVWLeIhA==&ch=pm-eKGb0hO5ls-Eqn8Zp6Im-kHoMRZyf-1_7HoEdpPuI1kpV3cd84A==).
---
|
---
| Clarity Roundtable Discussion Ally is determined to initiate unprecedented Clarity between her role as Senator in D.C. with her constituents in Utah. Ally invited communications experts from across the state to a roundtable discussion on how she can actively listen and share information in two-way communication with the people, families and businesses in Utah. Ally knows Utahns deserve this from their elected officials.
View the roundtable discussion [here](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001UV80nF325Xl6gYvWr5PqaUIQyUVLPhNIzu7IvWR9bycZzQDDhsWJjXBw1ggLsATSSgAQIzQL3XVMDpZJENBvGx9iyJeB5PonTPq6AOaQAISLiPRqLVmMjtb5LAmq7BLADRKoComTy_Yh254QwdcgysDShs1AQP8FLKZYMhnUQBhmKc6Z5M_ANKfNvrcrgT_eMCYyq8HVH3fXz3sPQ8mX2g==&c=I0nMU01PR6ekQjRFb6dBJvE1QYqpLe-IxT9QQnHHYUjvlvxVWLeIhA==&ch=pm-eKGb0hO5ls-Eqn8Zp6Im-kHoMRZyf-1_7HoEdpPuI1kpV3cd84A==).
---
|

clean_body: #AllyForUtah Update | The latest news from the Ally Isom For U.S. Senate Campaign | | | | | | | == | | Friend, Ally Isom has engaged in several important conversations about Clarity and inclusiveness lately. She was involved in a Black History Month conversation with women leaders and a Clarity Roundtable discussion with communication experts. KPCW radio's Leslie Thatcher also interviewed Ally. Finally, find out where you can go to sign a petition to get Ally's name on the June 2022 Republican Primary ballot. | == | Black History Month Commemoration Leaders from Women Who Succeed invited Ally to be part of a recorded discussion about the dreams, challenges and opportunities for Black Utahns. This was held to commemorate Black History Month. These conversations are important to Ally and one of the reasons she is running for the U.S. Senate. Ally deeply believes that we need approachable elected officials who actively engage with their constituents. The right things can only get done when all voices are at the table. Watch the full event == Learn why listening matters to Ally ==. | | KPCW Interview Ally chats with Leslie Thatcher from KPCW radio in Park City to discuss her campaign and explain why she entered the race against an incumbent. "It's time for good people to get off the sidelines and get into the conversation and invite all people to the table. That's what I've done my whole career," said Ally. "I've tried to build opportunities for people to come together to solve really thorny problems. And it all begins by talking." Listen to clips of the interview ==. | | Clarity Roundtable Discussion Ally is determined to initiate unprecedented Clarity between her role as Senator in D.C. with her constituents in Utah. Ally invited communications experts from across the state to a roundtable discussion on how she can actively listen and share information in two-way communication with the people, families and businesses in Utah. Ally knows Utahns deserve this from their elected officials. View the roundtable discussion ==. | | Signature Gathering Events Team Ally will be out gathering signatures at the following locations and times this month. Please come sign a petition to get Ally on the ballot. == \- Ogden, Wed. - Sat. 3 - 5 p.m. == \- Murray, Wed. - Sat. 3 - 5 p.m. == \- Orem, Mon. - Fri. 10 a.m. - 6 p.m. and Sat. 12 - 2 p.m. Other locations where you can sign a petition this weekend: Fri.
